memory.summary: The scholarship payout is too significant to ignore, yet my impulse to grab immediate fun is strong since no one else depends on me right now. I need to decide if the future financial relief outweighs today's solo enjoyment.
Starting now converts this into manageable friction rather than a last-minute crisis, and I can safely defer the fun without losing anything. The scholarship is worth the short-term effort.

desires: Prove to myself that I can finish the scholarship application without burning out again, even though my body is screaming for sleep.
Secure the financial stability of this award before it slips away due to my own exhaustion and lack of focus tomorrow.
Avoid looking incompetent or unreliable to my cohort if they see me stumble on this final administrative hurdle.

goals.description: Hit 'Submit' on the scholarship application immediately. It's at 100%—the remaining friction is just my anxiety talking. I need to close this loop now so my brain stops screaming about unfinished business and I don't have to face the uncertainty of tomorrow while exhausted.
goals.rationale: My achievement axis demands closure, not perfection. With a short planning horizon and high risk tolerance, delaying submission feeds my procrastination bias. The impulsive urge to scroll or sleep won't help if I panic later; submitting now satisfies the immediate need for control and removes the 'fraud' feeling. I proved I can do it (100%), now I must prove I can finish the process.

goals.description: Do NOT start the 'fun_now' game stream yet. My sleep discipline is low (3/10) and stress is moderate (4/10). Starting leisure now risks derailing my focus capacity (6/10) before the real work is done. I'll wait until after submission to validate the reward.
goals.rationale: Even though leisure has value, my current state prioritizes academic outcomes. Dropping this goal temporarily protects my momentum. If I switch to fun now, the dopamine hit might make the final polish of the essay feel unnecessary, leading to a re-open cycle I can't afford right now.

agent.last_reasoning: It's 06:15; I just hit 100%. Submitting now satisfies my achievement urge and stops the anxiety loop. Starting 'fun_now' feels like a waste of this perfect moment, but sleeping risks the deadline if I crash later. Waiting preserves the option to submit instantly when I'm fresh.

goals.description: Kill the intro text and upload the two documents for the scholarship immediately; I can't afford to lose this money.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis screams that missing a financial deadline is unacceptable failure. The risk tolerance is high, but the consequence of losing the award outweighs the dopamine hit from gaming. I need to secure the win now before my impulsive urge takes over.

goals.description: Go play the game or do the solo hobby for 45 minutes after the uploads are confirmed sent.
goals.rationale: I deserve immediate dopamine relief (impulsive time preference), but only after the high-value task is done. If I start playing now, I'll just procrastinate until the deadline hits and feel like an idiot. Better to finish strong and reward myself later.

desires: Secure the scholarship money before my impulsive urge to game wins out and I miss the deadline.
